text:
Part D, a beneficiary qualifies for catastrophic coverage when his/her true out-of-pocket 
(TrOOP) costs reach a specific dollar threshold (for example, the threshold was $4,550 in 2010). For 
prescriptions that were filled when a beneficiary was below the out-of-pocket threshold (OOPT), this 
field will contain a zero-dollar amount. For prescriptions above the OOPT, this field will contain a 
positive dollar value. If a prescription crosses the threshold, then the portion of the gross drug
